```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| include: false

# FUNCTIONS
split_by_species <- function(df, species_col = "species") {
  split(df, df[[species_col]])
}

correct_baseline <- function(df, l = 1*10^7, meta_in = TRUE) {
  # keep metadata
  meta <- df |> select(time_point, species, sample, enzyme, instrument)

  # apply baseline correction only to numeric columns
  numeric_df <- df |> select(where(is.numeric))
  numeric_corrected <- baseline.corr(numeric_df, lambda = l, p = 0.001, eps = 1e-8)

  if (meta_in) {
    combined <- bind_cols(meta, numeric_corrected)
    return(combined)
  }
  return(numeric_corrected)
}

plot_by_time <- function(df, title_input = "Title") {
  df |>
    filter(instrument == "DAD") |>
    mutate(sample_id = paste(enzyme, sample, time_point, species, sep = "_")) |>
    ggplot(
      aes(
        x = time,
        y = intensity,
        color = time_point,
        group = sample_id,
      )
    ) +
    geom_line() +
    scale_color_viridis_d(
      option = "viridis",
      name = "Incubation Time"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title_input,
      x = "Retention Time",
      y = "Intensity (au)",
      shape = "Species"
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
}

wide_to_long <- function(df) {
  df |>
    pivot_longer(
      cols = where(is.numeric),
      names_to = "time",
      values_to = "intensity"
    ) |>
    mutate(time = as.numeric(time))
}

pca <- function(df, num_comp = 10, center_in = TRUE, scale_in = TRUE, scale_na = FALSE) {
  df <- df |>
    mutate(time_point = factor(time_point, levels = c("0", "2", "5", "15", "30")))

  metadata <- df |>
    select(!where(is.numeric))

  if (scale_na) {
    dataPCA <- df |>
      select(where(is.numeric)) |>
      mutate(across(
        everything(),
        ~ ifelse(is.na(.), min(., na.rm = TRUE) / 2, .)
      ))
  } else {
    dataPCA <- df |>
      select(where(is.numeric))
  }

  pca_res <- prcomp(dataPCA, center = center_in, scale. = scale_in)

  scores <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x[, 1:num_comp, drop = FALSE])
  scores <- bind_cols(metadata, scores)
  loadings <- as.data.frame(pca_res$rotation[, 1:num_comp, drop = FALSE])
  variance <- data.frame(variance = (pca_res$sdev^2) / sum(pca_res$sdev^2))

  list(
    df = df,
    scores = scores,
    loadings = loadings,
    variance = variance
  )
}

plot_scores <- function(scores, PC_first, PC_second, title_in = "", file_path = NULL) {
  PC_first  <- rlang::enquo(PC_first)
  PC_second <- rlang::enquo(PC_second)

  circle_df <- scores |>
    group_by(species) |>
    summarize(
      x0 = mean(!!PC_first, na.rm = TRUE),
      y0 = mean(!!PC_second, na.rm = TRUE),
      r = max(
        sqrt((!!PC_first - x0)^2 + (!!PC_second - y0)^2),
        na.rm = TRUE
      ),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  label_positions <- circle_df |>
    mutate(
      dx = x0 / sqrt(x0^2 + y0^2),
      dy = y0 / sqrt(x0^2 + y0^2),
      x = x0 + dx * (r * 1.08),
      y = y0 + dy * (r * 1.08)
    )

  p <- ggplot(scores, aes(x = !!PC_first, y = !!PC_second)) +
    geom_point(
      aes(color = time_point, shape = species),
      size = 3
    ) +
    scale_color_viridis_d(
      option = "viridis",
      name = "Incubation Time"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title_in,
      subtitle = "Colored by incubation time; species-level dispersion shown",
      x = rlang::as_label(PC_first),
      y = rlang::as_label(PC_second),
      shape = "Species"
    ) +
    coord_equal() +
    theme_minimal()

  if (!missing(file_path) && !is.null(file_path)) {
    ggsave(
      filename = file_path,
      plot = last_plot(),
      dpi = 300,
      width = 8,
      height = 6,
      units = "in"
    )
  }
  return(p)
}

plot_loadings <- function(df, PC) {
  PC  <- rlang::enquo(PC)

  ggplot(df, aes(x = time, y = !!PC))+
    geom_line()+
    labs(title = "PCA Loadings Plot",
         x = "Time",
         y = rlang::as_label(PC)) +
    theme_minimal()
}

plot_scree <- function(df){
  df <- df |>
    mutate(
      PC = factor(
        paste0("PC", row_number()),
        levels = paste0("PC", row_number())
      )
    )

  df_new <- df |>
    mutate(cumulative = cumsum(variance)) |>
    slice(1:10)

  ggplot(df_new, aes(x = PC, y = variance, group = 1)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = .7) +
    geom_point(size = 1) +
    geom_text(data = df_new |> slice(1:4),
              inherit.aes = FALSE,
              aes(x = PC, y = variance, label = paste0(round(cumulative*100), "%")),
              vjust = -0.5, hjust = -.4, size = 3) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
    labs(
      title = "Scree Plot of Principal Components",
      x = "Principal Component",
      y = "Proportion of Variance Explained",
      caption = "Percentages indicate cumulative variance explained"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}

ptw_function <- function(df) {
  meta <- df |>
    select(time_point, species, sample, enzyme, instrument)

  # Use time_point=30, sample=m4, enzyme=CYP+UGT as the reference (maximum
  # incubation with combined enzyme — biologically meaningful anchor).
  # Falls back to the first row if that sample is absent for a given species.
  reference <- df |>
    dplyr::filter(time_point == 30, sample == "m4", enzyme == "CYP+UGT") |>
    dplyr::slice(1)

  if (nrow(reference) == 0) {
    reference <- df |> dplyr::slice(1)
  }

  samples <- df |>
    dplyr::anti_join(
      reference |> select(time_point, species, sample, enzyme, instrument),
      by = c("time_point", "species", "sample", "enzyme", "instrument")
    )

  reference_meta <- reference |> select(time_point, species, sample, enzyme, instrument)
  samples_meta   <- samples   |> select(time_point, species, sample, enzyme, instrument)

  reference_num <- reference |> select(where(is.numeric))
  samples_num   <- samples   |> select(where(is.numeric))

  res <- ptw(reference_num, samples_num, warp.type = "individual")

  warped_df    <- as.data.frame(res$warped.sample)
  reference_df <- as.data.frame(res$reference)

  colnames(warped_df) <- colnames(reference_df)

  final_df <- bind_rows(
    bind_cols(reference_meta, reference_df),
    bind_cols(samples_meta,   warped_df)
  )

  return(final_df)
}

scale_wide <- function(df) {
  meta <- df |> select(!where(is.numeric))
  numeric_df <- df |> select(where(is.numeric))
  scaled <- as.data.frame(scale(numeric_df, center = TRUE, scale = TRUE))
  scaled[is.nan(as.matrix(scaled))] <- 0
  bind_cols(meta, scaled)
}

snr_mask <- function(df, threshold = 5) {
  numeric_df <- df |> select(where(is.numeric))
  snr <- apply(numeric_df, 2, function(x) {
    max(x, na.rm = TRUE) / (IQR(x, na.rm = TRUE) + 1e-8)
  })
  keep <- names(snr)[snr > threshold]
  meta <- df |> select(!where(is.numeric))
  bind_cols(meta, numeric_df |> select(all_of(keep)))
}

max_mask <- function(df, threshold = 20) {
  numeric_df <- df |> select(where(is.numeric))
  max_signal <- apply(numeric_df, 2, max, na.rm = TRUE)
  keep <- names(max_signal)[max_signal > threshold]
  meta <- df |> select(!where(is.numeric))
  bind_cols(meta, numeric_df |> select(all_of(keep)))
}

# Returns a data frame of contiguous masked retention-time ranges for geom_rect
find_masked_ranges <- function(df, mask_fn, ...) {
  all_times  <- sort(as.numeric(names(df  |> select(where(is.numeric)))))
  kept_times <- sort(as.numeric(names(mask_fn(df, ...) |> select(where(is.numeric)))))
  masked <- setdiff(all_times, kept_times)
  if (length(masked) == 0) return(data.frame(xmin = numeric(0), xmax = numeric(0)))
  avg_gap <- median(diff(all_times))
  breaks  <- which(diff(masked) > avg_gap * 2)
  data.frame(
    xmin = c(masked[1],          masked[breaks + 1]),
    xmax = c(masked[breaks],     masked[length(masked)])
  )
}

normalize_rows <- function(df) {
  meta <- df |> select(!where(is.numeric))
  nums <- df |> select(where(is.numeric))
  bind_cols(meta, sweep(nums, 1, rowSums(nums, na.rm = TRUE), "/"))
}

peak_ranges <- function(df, min_height = 0.25, min_time_distance = 0.1, expand_frac = 0.05) {
  library(pracma)
  df <- df |> dplyr::arrange(time)
  dt <- median(diff(df$time))
  min_distance_rows <- round(min_time_distance / dt)

  p <- findpeaks(df$intensity, minpeakheight = min_height, minpeakdistance = min_distance_rows)
  if (is.null(p)) return(tibble::tibble())

  apex_indices <- sort(p[, 2])

  peak_ranges_list <- vector("list", length(apex_indices))
  for (i in seq_along(apex_indices)) {
    apex_idx  <- apex_indices[i]
    threshold <- df$intensity[apex_idx] * expand_frac
    start_idx <- apex_idx
    while (start_idx > 1 && df$intensity[start_idx] > threshold) start_idx <- start_idx - 1
    if (i > 1) start_idx <- max(start_idx, apex_indices[i - 1] + 1)
    end_idx <- apex_idx
    while (end_idx < nrow(df) && df$intensity[end_idx] > threshold) end_idx <- end_idx + 1
    if (i < length(apex_indices)) end_idx <- min(end_idx, apex_indices[i + 1] - 1)
    peak_ranges_list[[i]] <- c(start_idx, end_idx)
  }

  peak_ranges_mat <- do.call(rbind, peak_ranges_list)
  t <- tibble::tibble(
    start_time  = df$time[peak_ranges_mat[, 1]],
    apex_time   = df$time[apex_indices],
    end_time    = df$time[peak_ranges_mat[, 2]],
    peak_height = p[order(apex_indices), 1]
  )
  if (nrow(t) == 0) return(t)

  merged <- list(t[1, ])
  for (i in 2:nrow(t)) {
    prev <- merged[[length(merged)]]
    if (t$apex_time[i] - prev$apex_time < 0.02) {
      merged[[length(merged)]] <- tibble::tibble(
        start_time  = min(prev$start_time, t$start_time[i]),
        apex_time   = if (t$peak_height[i] > prev$peak_height) t$apex_time[i] else prev$apex_time,
        end_time    = max(prev$end_time, t$end_time[i]),
        peak_height = max(prev$peak_height, t$peak_height[i])
      )
    } else {
      merged <- append(merged, list(t[i, ]))
    }
  }

  do.call(rbind, merged) |>
    dplyr::mutate(peak_group = dplyr::row_number()) |>
    dplyr::select(peak_group, dplyr::everything())
}

riemann_integrate <- function(df, rt1, rt2) {
  df |>
    dplyr::filter(time >= rt1, time <= rt2) |>
    dplyr::arrange(time) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      dt            = time - dplyr::lag(time),
      intensity_mid = (intensity + dplyr::lag(intensity)) / 2,
      area          = dt * intensity_mid
    ) |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(area)) |>
    dplyr::summarise(area = sum(area)) |>
    dplyr::pull(area)
}

compute_volcano_stats <- function(wide_df) {
  long_df <- wide_to_long(wide_df) |>
    dplyr::filter(instrument == "DAD") |>
    dplyr::mutate(time_point = as.character(time_point))

  # Detect peaks from mean chromatogram per species/enzyme
  ref <- long_df |>
    dplyr::group_by(species, enzyme, time) |>
    dplyr::summarize(intensity = mean(intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  raw_peaks <- ref |>
    dplyr::group_by(species, enzyme) |>
    dplyr::group_modify(~ peak_ranges(.x)) |>
    dplyr::ungroup()

  # Collapse peaks within 0.15 RT units per species/enzyme
  collapsed_peaks <- raw_peaks |>
    dplyr::group_by(species, enzyme) |>
    dplyr::arrange(apex_time) |>
    dplyr::mutate(peak_group2 = cumsum(c(TRUE, diff(apex_time) > 0.15))) |>
    dplyr::group_by(species, enzyme, peak_group2) |>
    dplyr::summarize(
      start_time  = min(start_time),
      apex_time   = apex_time[which.max(peak_height)],
      end_time    = max(end_time),
      peak_height = max(peak_height),
      .groups     = "drop"
    ) |>
    dplyr::rename(peak_number = peak_group2)

  # Integrate each peak per sample/timepoint
  peak_areas <- long_df |>
    dplyr::group_by(species, enzyme, time_point, sample) |>
    dplyr::group_modify(~ {
      sp   <- .y$species
      enz  <- .y$enzyme
      pkls <- collapsed_peaks |> dplyr::filter(species == sp, enzyme == enz)
      if (nrow(pkls) == 0) return(tibble::tibble())
      purrr::map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(pkls)), function(i) {
        pk <- pkls[i, ]
        tibble::tibble(
          peak_number = pk$peak_number,
          apex_time   = pk$apex_time,
          area        = riemann_integrate(.x, pk$start_time, pk$end_time)
        )
      })
    }) |>
    dplyr::ungroup()

  # Compute volcano stats: t-test of area vs baseline (time_point == "0")
  baseline_areas <- peak_areas |>
    dplyr::filter(time_point == "0") |>
    dplyr::select(species, enzyme, peak_number, apex_time, sample, base_area = area)

  peak_areas |>
    dplyr::filter(time_point != "0") |>
    dplyr::left_join(
      baseline_areas |> dplyr::select(-sample),
      by = c("species", "enzyme", "peak_number", "apex_time"),
      relationship = "many-to-many"
    ) |>
    dplyr::group_by(species, enzyme, peak_number, apex_time, time_point) |>
    dplyr::summarize(
      fc      = mean(area, na.rm = TRUE) / (mean(base_area, na.rm = TRUE) + 1e-8),
      p_value = tryCatch(
        t.test(area, base_area)$p.value,
        error = function(e) NA_real_
      ),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      log2fc     = log2(fc + 1e-8),
      neg_log10p = -log10(p_value),
      status     = dplyr::case_when(
        log2fc >  0.4 & !is.na(p_value) & p_value < 0.05 ~ "Up Regulated",
        log2fc < -0.4 & !is.na(p_value) & p_value < 0.05 ~ "Down Regulated",
        TRUE ~ "Insignificant"
      ),
      rt_label = paste0(round(apex_time, 2), " RT ", time_point, " min"),
      delabel  = dplyr::if_else(status != "Insignificant", rt_label, NA_character_)
    )
}

plot_volcano <- function(stats_df, title_in = "") {
  ggplot(stats_df, aes(x = log2fc, y = neg_log10p, color = status)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1.5) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.4, 0.4), col = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), col = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_text_repel(aes(label = delabel), show.legend = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Up Regulated" = "red", "Down Regulated" = "blue", "Insignificant" = "grey"),
      name = "Group"
    ) +
    labs(
      title    = title_in,
      subtitle = "Fold Change Compared to Zero Time Point",
      x        = "log2(FoldChange)",
      y        = "-log10(p-value)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.title.x  = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.title.y  = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.title  = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.text   = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
}

# LIBRARY LOADS AND DATA IMPORT
library(arrow)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
library(here)
library(ptw)
library(ggrepel)
library(pracma)

# Tab one baseline correction
combinedWide <- read_parquet("data/prepared/combinedWide.parquet")
combinedWide <- combinedWide |>
  mutate(time_point = factor(time_point, levels = c("0", "2", "5", "15", "30")))

combinedLong <- read_parquet("data/prepared/combinedLong.parquet")
combinedLong <- combinedLong |>
  mutate(time_point = factor(time_point, levels = c("0", "2", "5", "15", "30")))
```

```{r}
library(arrow)
library(tidyverse) # for data manipulation and visualization
library(patchwork) # for combining plots
library(here) # for file path management
library(ptw)
library(ggrepel)

#Tab one baseline correction
combinedWide <- read_parquet("data/prepared/combinedWide.parquet")
combinedWide <- combinedWide |> 
  mutate(time_point = factor(time_point, levels = c("0", "2", "5", "15", "30")))
combinedLong <- read_parquet("data/prepared/combinedLong.parquet")
combinedLong <- combinedLong |> 
  mutate(time_point = factor(time_point, levels = c("0", "2", "5", "15", "30")))
```

## {#chemometrics background-color="#533860"}

::: {style="color: white; font-size: 1.8em; font-weight: bold;"}
🌿 Chemometrics & Metabolomics
:::
::: {style="color: #FFE42A; font-size: 1.1em;"}
Statistics meets plant chemistry
:::

## Undergraduate Collaborator

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
- This work completed in conjunction with Aiden Everard
- Junior at College of Idaho
:::
::: {.column}
![](../images/HeadShot.jpg)
:::
::::

## Ecology {.smaller}

Sagebrush is vast

- There are many species of sagebrush
- Each subspecies of sagebrush has a unqiue chemical profile.

Sagegrouse diet is in large part sagebrush

- Enzymes in the grouse liver modify defensive chemicals.

Reasearch question

- Which compounds are present in certain species of Sagebrush but not other that the Sagegrouse enzymes ALSO interact with

## Experiment

- Collect samples from different species of sagebrush
- Treat samples with different grouse liver enzymes
- Apply Liquid Chromatography - Mass Spectrometry (LCMS) to extract chemical profile at different incubation times


## Liquid Chromatography {#lcms-intro}

![The LC column — heart of the separation step](images/LC_Column.jpeg){fig-align="center" height="420px"}

- Each sample exits at a different **retention time**

## What does the data look like?

```{r}
combinedLong |> 
  filter(instrument == "DAD") |> 
  mutate(sample_id = paste(enzyme, sample, time_point, species, sep = "_")) |>
    ggplot(
      aes(
        x = time,
        y = intensity,
        color = time_point,
        group = sample_id,
    ))+
    geom_line(alpha = .7)+
    facet_wrap(~species)+
    scale_color_viridis_d(
      option = "viridis",
      name = "Incubation Time"
      )+
    labs(
      title = "Raw Chromatogram of Each Species",
      x = "Retention Time",
      y = "Intensity (au)"
    )+
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), 
      axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),         
      axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"))
  
```

## Mass Spectrometry

- Compounds are broken apart and ionized
- Detector records each fragment's **mass-to-charge ratio**
- Intensity = how much of that fragment is present

## LC-MS Data: Questions {#lcms-data .smaller}

**One plant sample → one enormous table**

- Thousands of retention time points
- Thousands of mass values
- Result: **100,000+ features per sample**

## Data Analysis Problems

- Far more features than samples
- Lot's of (potential) preprocessing steps
- Data Integration
- Main differences in species aren't what we're interested in

## Baseline Correction {#preprocessing .smaller .scrollable}

- Instrument reads a "floor" even with no signal
- Asymmetric least squares fits and removes this floor
- Think: subtract a wavy background from a photo

$$
\min_{z} \; \sum_{i=1}^{n} w_i \, (y_i - z_i)^2 \;+\; \lambda \sum_{i=2}^{n-1} (z_{i-1} - 2 z_i + z_{i+1})^2
$$

where:

- $y_i$ : observed signal at point $i$  
- $z_i$ : estimated baseline at point $i$  
- $w_i$ : asymmetric weights, updated iteratively:

$$
w_i =
\begin{cases} 
p, & \text{if } y_i > z_i \\[2mm]
1 - p, & \text{if } y_i \le z_i
\end{cases}
$$

- $\lambda$ : smoothing parameter controlling baseline smoothness  
- $p$ : asymmetry parameter (small values keep the baseline below peaks)  

## Baseline Correction

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Green
```{r}
species_df = split_by_species(combinedWide)

green <- species_df[['green']]
greenMeta <-  green |> select(1:5)

greenWide <- correct_baseline(green, 1*10^9) 

greenLong <- wide_to_long(greenWide)
greenLong_raw <- wide_to_long(green)

plot_by_time(greenLong, title_input = "Baseline Corrected")
plot_by_time(greenLong_raw, title_input = "Raw Spectra")
```

### White
```{r}
white <- species_df[['white']]
whiteMeta <-  white |> select(1:5)

whiteWide <- correct_baseline(white, 1*10^9) 

whiteLong <- wide_to_long(whiteWide)
whiteLong_raw <- wide_to_long(white)

plot_by_time(whiteLong, title_input = "Baseline Corrected")
plot_by_time(whiteLong_raw, title_input = "Raw Spectra")
```

### Early
```{r}
 
early <- species_df[['early']]
earlyMeta <-  early |> select(1:5)

earlyWide <- correct_baseline(early, 1*10^9) 

earlyLong <- wide_to_long(earlyWide)
earlyLong_raw <- wide_to_long(early)

plot_by_time(earlyLong, title_input = "Baseline Corrected")
plot_by_time(earlyLong_raw, title_input = "Raw Spectra")
```

### Wyoming
```{r}

wyoming <- species_df[['wyoming']]
wyomingMeta <-  wyoming |> select(1:5)

wyomingWide <- correct_baseline(wyoming, 1*10^9) 

wyomingLong <- wide_to_long(wyomingWide)
wyomingLong_raw <- wide_to_long(wyoming)

plot_by_time(wyomingLong, title_input = "Baseline Corrected")
plot_by_time(wyomingLong_raw, title_input = "Raw Spectra")
```
:::

## Peak Alignment {#peak-alignment}

- Parametric time-warping
- Same compound exits at slightly different times across runs
- Warping stretches/compresses the time axis to align peaks
- Think: syncing two audio tracks that drifted out of time
- Question: how do you align different species to one another?

## Parametric Time-Warping

- Warping function $w(t)$: Polynomial of degree $K$
- Goal: align sample $S(t)$ to a reference signal $R(t)$ by "warping" time: $S(w(t))$

## Alignment
::: {.panel-tabset}
```{r}
greenPTW <- read_parquet("data/processed/greenPTW.parquet")
whitePTW <- read_parquet("data/processed/whitePTW.parquet")
earlyPTW <- read_parquet("data/processed/earlyPTW.parquet")
wyomingPTW <- read_parquet("data/processed/wyomingPTW.parquet")
totalWide_PTW <- bind_rows(greenPTW, earlyPTW, whitePTW, wyomingPTW) |> 
  mutate(time_point = factor(time_point, levels = c("0", "2", "5", "15", "30")))
greenLong_PTW <- wide_to_long(greenPTW)
whiteLong_PTW <- wide_to_long(whitePTW)
earlyLong_PTW <- wide_to_long(earlyPTW)
wyomingLong_PTW <- wide_to_long(wyomingPTW)

greenLong_PTW <- greenLong_PTW |> 
  mutate(time_point = factor(time_point, levels = c("0", "2", "5", "15", "30")))

whiteLong_PTW <- whiteLong_PTW |> 
  mutate(time_point = factor(time_point, levels = c("0", "2", "5", "15", "30")))

earlyLong_PTW <- earlyLong_PTW |> 
  mutate(time_point = factor(time_point, levels = c("0", "2", "5", "15", "30")))

wyomingLong_PTW <- wyomingLong_PTW |> 
  mutate(time_point = factor(time_point, levels = c("0", "2", "5", "15", "30")))
```

### Green
```{r}
plot_by_time(greenLong_PTW)
```

### White
```{r}
plot_by_time(whiteLong_PTW)
```

### Early
```{r}
plot_by_time(earlyLong_PTW)
```

### Wyoming
```{r}
plot_by_time(wyomingLong_PTW)
```

:::

## Standardizing

- Typically: center and scale data
- Problem: Low concentration compounds blow-up
- Potential solutions:
  - Create mask based on signal-to-noise ratio
  - Create mask for any peaks above threshold


## Centering & Scaling

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| include: false
ptw_split    <- split_by_species(totalWide_PTW)
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Green
```{r}
plot_by_time(greenLong_PTW, title_input = "PTW-Aligned — Green")
```

### White
```{r}
plot_by_time(whiteLong_PTW, title_input = "PTW-Aligned — White")
```

### Early
```{r}
plot_by_time(earlyLong_PTW, title_input = "PTW-Aligned — Early")
```

### Wyoming
```{r}
plot_by_time(wyomingLong_PTW, title_input = "PTW-Aligned — Wyoming")
```

:::

## SNR Mask + Scale

Gray = masked regions (SNR ≤ 3); SNR = max signal / IQR

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| include: false
snr_ranges <- find_masked_ranges(totalWide_PTW, snr_mask, threshold = 3)
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Green
```{r}
plot_by_time(greenLong_PTW, title_input = "SNR Mask (SNR > 3) — Green") +
  geom_rect(data = snr_ranges,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.3)
```

### White
```{r}
plot_by_time(whiteLong_PTW, title_input = "SNR Mask (SNR > 3) — White") +
  geom_rect(data = snr_ranges,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.3)
```

### Early
```{r}
plot_by_time(earlyLong_PTW, title_input = "SNR Mask (SNR > 3) — Early") +
  geom_rect(data = snr_ranges,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.3)
```

### Wyoming
```{r}
plot_by_time(wyomingLong_PTW, title_input = "SNR Mask (SNR > 3) — Wyoming") +
  geom_rect(data = snr_ranges,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.3)
```

:::

## Peak Threshold Mask + Scale

Gray = masked regions (max signal ≤ 10 AU)

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| include: false
max_ranges <- find_masked_ranges(totalWide_PTW, max_mask, threshold = 10)
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Green
```{r}
plot_by_time(greenLong_PTW, title_input = "Peak Threshold Mask (max > 10) — Green") +
  geom_rect(data = max_ranges,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.3)
```

### White
```{r}
plot_by_time(whiteLong_PTW, title_input = "Peak Threshold Mask (max > 10) — White") +
  geom_rect(data = max_ranges,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.3)
```

### Early
```{r}
plot_by_time(earlyLong_PTW, title_input = "Peak Threshold Mask (max > 10) — Early") +
  geom_rect(data = max_ranges,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.3)
```

### Wyoming
```{r}
plot_by_time(wyomingLong_PTW, title_input = "Peak Threshold Mask (max > 10) — Wyoming") +
  geom_rect(data = max_ranges,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.3)
```

:::

## Normalization {#preprocessing-normalize}

- Samples may have different total amounts loaded
- Scale each sample so totals are comparable
- Collaborators disagree on whether do this


## PCA: Unnormalized

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| include: false
pca_unnorm <- pca(max_mask(totalWide_PTW, threshold = 10))
```

```{r}
plot_scores(pca_unnorm$scores, PC1, PC2,
            title_in = "PCA Scores — Max Threshold, Unnormalized") |
plot_scree(pca_unnorm$variance)
```

## PCA: Row Normalized

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| include: false
pca_norm <- pca(normalize_rows(max_mask(totalWide_PTW, threshold = 10)))
```

```{r}
plot_scores(pca_norm$scores, PC1, PC2,
            title_in = "PCA Scores — Max Threshold, Row Normalized") |
plot_scree(pca_norm$variance)
```

## Volcano Plots: CYP Enzyme

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| eval: true
#| include: false
volcano_stats <- compute_volcano_stats(max_mask(totalWide_PTW, threshold = 10))
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Green
```{r}
volcano_stats |>
  dplyr::filter(enzyme == "CYP", species == "green") |>
  plot_volcano(title_in = "Green — CYP Enzyme")
```

### White
```{r}
volcano_stats |>
  dplyr::filter(enzyme == "CYP", species == "white") |>
  plot_volcano(title_in = "White — CYP Enzyme")
```

### Early
```{r}
volcano_stats |>
  dplyr::filter(enzyme == "CYP", species == "early") |>
  plot_volcano(title_in = "Early — CYP Enzyme")
```

### Wyoming
```{r}
volcano_stats |>
  dplyr::filter(enzyme == "CYP", species == "wyoming") |>
  plot_volcano(title_in = "Wyoming — CYP Enzyme")
```

:::


## Collaborators {#chemometrics-collab}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**College of Idaho**

- **Aiden Everard** — Undergraduate
- **Carolyn Dadabay** — Chemistry Professor

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Boise State University**

- **Jenn Forby** — Biology Professor
- **Maddy Skinner** — Master's Student
:::

::::

::: {.fragment}
*Undergraduate-driven research: from raw LC-MS data to publishable analysis*
:::

---

[← Back to Topics](#toc)
